texts.content:
From: Career Services
To: Senior Class
Subject: Spring Career Fair — Register Now!

Dear Seniors,

The Spring Career Fair will be held on March 25 from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM in the Student Union Ballroom. Over 50 employers from various industries will be in attendance.

Registration is required. Please sign up through the Career Services portal by March 20 to receive a personalized schedule. Bring at least 10 copies of your resume and prepare a brief introduction about your skills and career goals.

Professional attire is required. First 100 registered students will receive a free professional headshot.

questions.stem: What is the email asking students to do?
options: A. Submit their resumes for review
B. Register for an upcoming career fair
C. Schedule individual meetings with employers
D. Prepare a presentation about their career goals
questions.explanation: The email announces the career fair and instructs students to 'sign up through the Career Services portal' by March 20.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the registration deadline?
options: A. Students can register at the door on the day of the event
B. Registering by March 20 provides additional benefits
C. Late registrations are accepted with a fee
D. Only seniors who register early can attend
questions.explanation: Registration by March 20 is linked to receiving 'a personalized schedule' and being among the first 100 to get a free headshot, suggesting benefits for early registration.

questions.stem: How many copies of their resume are students advised to bring?
options: A. At least 5 copies
B. At least 10 copies
C. At least 15 copies
D. At least 20 copies
questions.explanation: The email advises students to 'Bring at least 10 copies of your resume.'

texts.content:
City Marathon — Race Day Schedule

Sunday, October 12

6:00 AM — Runners' check-in opens (Main Square)
6:30 AM — Bag drop closes
7:00 AM — Wheelchair race starts
7:15 AM — Full marathon (26.2 miles) starts
7:30 AM — Half marathon (13.1 miles) starts
8:00 AM — 5K fun run starts
9:30 AM — Water stations close for full marathon
11:00 AM — Award ceremony begins
1:00 PM — Course officially closes

Note: Road closures begin at 5:30 AM and are expected to lift by 2:00 PM.

questions.stem: How much later does the half marathon start compared to the full marathon?
options: A. 15 minutes
B. 30 minutes
C. 45 minutes
D. 60 minutes
questions.explanation: The full marathon starts at 7:15 AM and the half marathon starts at 7:30 AM — a difference of 15 minutes.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about runners who have not finished the marathon by 1:00 PM?
options: A. They will receive a medal regardless
B. They must move to the sidewalk and follow traffic rules
C. They will be redirected to the shorter course
D. They can continue but will not have water station support
questions.explanation: Since the course officially closes at 1:00 PM and road closures lift by 2:00 PM, runners still on the course would need to use pedestrian routes.

texts.content:
Important: Annual Fire Drill

On Thursday, November 14 at 10:00 AM, we will conduct our annual building-wide fire drill. When the alarm sounds, please do the following:

1. Immediately stop what you are doing.
2. Close any open windows and doors behind you.
3. Walk — do not run — to the nearest emergency exit.
4. Proceed to the designated assembly area in the north parking lot.
5. Wait for further instructions from the safety warden.

Do not use elevators. Do not re-enter the building until the all-clear signal is given.

The drill is expected to last approximately 15 minutes.

questions.stem: What is the primary reason for this notice?
options: A. To report a fire incident that occurred
B. To inform occupants about an upcoming safety exercise
C. To announce new fire safety equipment installation
D. To update the building's emergency evacuation plan
questions.explanation: The notice informs building occupants about the 'annual building-wide fire drill' on a specific date and provides evacuation instructions.

questions.stem: The instruction 'do not re-enter the building until the all-clear signal is given' suggests that:
options: A. the alarm will continue until everyone has left the building
B. a specific signal will indicate when it is safe to return
C. the drill will end automatically after 15 minutes
D. the safety warden will escort everyone back inside
questions.explanation: The phrase 'all-clear signal' implies there is a designated signal that occupants must wait for before re-entering.
